prompt:
A single, powerful conceptual image representing climate change over time as a physical metaphor.

Scene: A long perspective view of thousands of matchsticks arranged in neat rows, stretching from the foreground into the distance like a timeline.

At the far end (past): the matchsticks are intact, clean, and unlit, pale wood tones.

Moving toward the viewer: the matchsticks gradually become charred, then partially burned.

In the foreground (present): the matchsticks are actively burning with bright orange flames, collapsing, blackened, and turning to ash. Some have already disintegrated.

The transition from intact to burning should feel gradual but accelerating — very few burning sticks in the distance, many in the foreground.

Lighting: cinematic, dark background, strong contrast, glowing fire illuminating nearby sticks.

Mood: somber, urgent, quiet but devastating.

Composition: single frame, no text, no labels, no numbers, no charts. The meaning must be understood visually.

Style: hyper-realistic, high detail, magazine cover quality, shallow depth of field, sharp focus on foreground flames.

Negative prompt: no globe, no thermometer, no graphs, no charts, no text, no infographic style, no labels, no split panels
dataset_name: NASA GISTEMP Global Surface Temperature Anomalies
insight: Recent decades show a sharp and accelerating increase in global temperatures compared to the past.
